page: Amazon Web Services r5dn.12xlarge is a memory-optimized instance built on the Nitro hypervisor. It features 48 vCPUs and 24 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor operating at 3.1 GHz. The x86_64 architecture includes 35 MB of L3 cache and supports AVX-512 vector extensions, AVX512_VNNI, and AES cryptography. The instance is equipped with 384.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, providing a memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core.

This instance includes 1800 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and features a baseline network bandwidth of 50 Gbps. The combination of high-speed local storage and high network throughput supports data-intensive operations. No GPUs are included in this configuration.

Benchmark results show average performance across most multi-core and memory operations. The stress-ng multi-core CPU performance, PassMark CPU score, and PassMark database operations all fall within the average tier. However, single-core performance is a notable tradeoff, with stress-ng single-core CPU performance and single-threaded gzip compression scoring in the weak tier. Large-model LLM inference is also limited, with prompt processing for a 70B model rated as weak.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the r5dn.12xlarge offers a high-density memory profile paired with fast local NVMe storage and high network bandwidth, making it suitable for memory-bound workloads that require rapid local data access. Appropriate workloads include database operations, memory-intensive data processing, and caching layers like Redis, where its average multi-core and memory bandwidth capabilities can be fully utilized.
description: Amazon Web Services r5dn.12xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 48 vCPUs and 24 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor running at 3.1 GHz. It is equipped with 384.0 GB of DDR4 memory, 1800 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, and a baseline network bandwidth of 50 Gbps. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, the instance supports AVX-512 instructions. Benchmark data indicates average performance for multi-core CPU tasks, memory bandwidth, and database operations, though single-core performance and single-threaded compression tasks are weak. The instance lacks GPU acceleration. This hardware profile is designed for memory-intensive workloads, database operations, and applications requiring high network throughput and local NVMe storage.
